Investor Anatomy
Global Trade and Demand
Home
Global Trade and Demand
Assesses global trade intensity and capital flows by combining USD regime signals, macro positioning (for example, supply stress), and speculative risk tone.

Regime Assessment:

  • Expansionary / Risk-Positive
  • Regime Confidence Index: Medium Confidence
  • The regime is fragile.

Why This Regime:

  • The primary driver is the Strong Risk-On positioning tone, which maintains a Z-score of 1.54, well above expansionary thresholds (1).
  • Dynamic weighting prioritizes the tactical High-Weight, High-Confidence Tone signal over mixed cyclical indicators (1).
  • The USD Index remains structurally below its 12-month moving average, supporting global risk appetite (2).
  • Macro themes were downweighted due to mixed signals and internal conflict, as speculative capital currently lacks a clear sectoral destination (3).

Alignment & Tensions:

  • Extreme speculative long alignment reinforces the tailwind provided by a structurally weak USD (1)(2).
  • Tension exists between high tactical appetite and the sharp collapse in Energy speculative interest, which prevents a broader inflationary impulse (3).
  • These tensions do not overturn the call because the core positioning appetite remains persistent and lacks broad liquidation signs (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Dominant scenario: Continued expansionary bias driven by trend continuation in speculative flows.
  • Primary upside risk: Acceleration of risk-on momentum if crowded shorts capitulate in lagging sectors.
  • Primary downside risk: Exhaustion of the Risk-On regime if USD momentum turns reinforcingly positive.

What Would Change the Regime:

  • The Global Positioning Tone Z-score falling below 1.0 would signal a shift to Neutral or Risk-Off (1).
  • A monthly USD close above the 12-month moving average of 120.27 would invalidate the structural bearish tailwind (2).
  • An Energy hedger deep long share exceeding 20% would trigger a Supply Stress regime (3).
Inputs
Global Risk-On / Risk-Off Positioning Tone

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a CoT Market Tone Z-score of 1.54, a crowded long share of 26.89%, and a net tone of 0.064, this setup aligns with Upward-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Trend continuation, not mean reversion.
  • The signal is currently in a persistent Strong Risk-On regime, reflecting extreme speculative long alignment (1).
  • Conviction Band: High; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Stable; Threshold Proximity: Moderate; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Robust Z-Scores ≥ 1.0 signify a Strong Risk-On regime characterized by extreme speculative long alignment (1).
  • Positive net tone values indicate an expansionary bias, while negative values indicate a contractionary bias (1).
  • The signal uses speculative futures positioning and crowding intensity as primary drivers to quantify aggregate risk appetite (1).
  • Global Risk-On / Risk-Off Positioning Tone: Latest observation 2026-03-10 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The current state is driven by a Z-score of 1.54, which remains well above the 1.0 threshold for Strong Risk-On conditions (1).
  • Positioning exhibits high persistence, maintaining the current regime for six consecutive weeks (1).
  • Momentum has stabilized following a peak in crowding intensity (Z = 1.97) in mid-February (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: The regime would shift to "Risk-On" or "Neutral" if the CoT Market Tone Z-score falls below 1.0 (1).
  • Signal stability is high, as speculative appetite shows no immediate signs of broad liquidation (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Continued expansionary bias supported by aggressive speculative long alignment.
  • Upside secondary: Further risk-on acceleration if crowded shorts capitulate in lagging sectors.
  • Downside residual: Sharp reversal or exhaustion risk if net tone momentum turns negative.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks): The signal reflects weekly CoT updates and identifies short-term squeeze or exhaustion risks (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: High, as it serves as the primary sentiment filter for macro risk allocation (1).

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the global sentiment and speculative flow dimension of macro aggregation (1).
  • Implied economic mechanism: Broad speculative appetite for pro-cyclical risk exposure across futures markets (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typically interacts with VIX and Credit Spreads to confirm regime transitions (1).

Regime Context:

  • The current Strong Risk-On regime has been persistent since February 2026 (1).
  • Direction of change: Stabilizing at extreme levels after a slight weakening from mid-February peaks (1).

Model Limitations:

  • Masks sector-level divergence and is highly sensitive to liquidity in thin markets (1).
  • Static thresholds may lag significant structural macro shifts (1).

Data & References:

  • Global Risk-On / Risk-Off Positioning Tone (CoT): 2026-03-10 (1).
  • Influential datapoints: Z-score (1.54), Crowded Long Share (26.89%) (1).
  • Public datasets for improvement: CBOE VIX Index, ICE BofA Credit Spreads.
Regime-Specific Macro Themes

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given the 16% speculative long share in Energy and 37.5% hedger deep short share in Metals, this setup aligns with Indeterminate price paths and Indeterminate volatility, where the dominant risk is Data uncertainty, not a confirmed macro theme (1).
  • The signal is currently in a non-consensus/transitional state as no formal regime thresholds for Inflation Impulse, Disinflation, or Global Slowdown are met.
  • Conviction Band: Low; Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes. Signal Stability Assessment: Volatile; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Inflation Impulse requires concurrent speculative crowding in Energy (≥40%) and Metals (≥30%) (1).
  • Supply Stress is triggered when commercial hedgers are deep long in Energy or Ags (≥20%) (1).
  • Global Slowdown requires Metals speculative short crowding (≥35%) and broad Risk-Off positioning (1).
  • Regime-Specific Macro Themes (Latest: 2026-03-10) (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • Energy speculative interest collapsed from 32% to 16% in one week, preventing an Inflation Impulse trigger (1).
  • Agricultural markets show the highest speculative crowding (46%), but lack the necessary cross-sector breadth for a global regime shift (1).
  • Internal Tension: Metals hedgers are deep short (37.5%), implying supply expansion, while Energy hedgers are nearly deep long (19.3%), nearing a Supply Stress trigger (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: An increase in Energy hedger deep long share above 20% would activate the Supply Stress regime.
  • Signal stability is low following the sharp liquidation of Energy longs.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant; risks balanced due to the lack of threshold breach.
  • Most plausible upside risk: Supply Stress via a 0.7% increase in Energy hedger long concentration.
  • Most plausible downside risk: Global Slowdown if Metals speculative shorts (currently 0%) begin to aggregate.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) based on the multi-week persistence of CoT positioning shifts.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Low, as the signal is currently transitional and lacks consensus alignment.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs Global Growth & Economic Cycles by identifying the dominant sector-led impulse.
  • The current state implies a Liquidity pause or transition, where speculative capital is rotating out of Energy without finding a clear macro destination (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Interacts with USD strength and VIX levels to confirm risk-off clusters.

Regime Context:

  • Persistent non-consensus state; the signal has failed to trigger a primary regime for three consecutive reports.
  • Direction of change: Weakening speculative conviction in pro-cyclical sectors.

Model Limitations:

  • Static thresholds may lag cycle shifts.
  • Binary flags do not capture the intensity of the underlying macro theme.

Data & References:

  • Regime-Specific Macro Themes (2026-03-10) (1).
  • Most influential: Energy spec long share (0.16) and Metals hedger short share (0.375).
  • Secondary verification via Credit Spreads or VIX would improve regime clarity.
USD Index Signal

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a USD index level of 119.49 being below its 12-month moving average of 120.27 and a 6-month change of -0.54%, this setup aligns with Transition-biased price paths and Indeterminate volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not trend continuation (1).
  • The signal is currently classified as Bearish, representing a persistent regime that has endured for 12 months, though it is currently weakening as momentum loses its negative thrust (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: Mixed Signals; Internal Conflict Flag: Yes. Signal Stability Assessment: Deteriorating; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • USD levels below the 12-month moving average (MA) generally indicate a Bearish regime, acting as a proxy for easing financial conditions (1).
  • Reinforcing downside momentum requires a 6-month change below -5%; values between -5% and 0% suggest a transition state (1).
  • A conflict between level (below MA) and momentum (fading negative change) identifies an indeterminate or transition regime (1).
  • USD Index Signal Dataset (latest observation: 2026-03-01) (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the monthly value (119.49) remaining below the 12-month MA (120.27), maintaining a structural bearish bias (1).
  • Momentum has stabilized significantly, moving from a peak negative thrust of -7.63% in June 2025 to just -0.54% currently, indicating a loss of bearish conviction (1).
  • Internal Tension: The dataset maintains a "Bearish" label despite momentum failing to meet the -5% reinforcing threshold defined in the methodology (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A monthly close above the 12-month MA (currently 120.27) would confirm a regime shift to Neutral or Bullish (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued transition toward a Neutral regime as price and the 12-month MA converge.
  • Most plausible upside risk: A sharp reversal in global risk appetite driving safe-haven demand and a return to Bullish status (>5% momentum).
  • Most plausible downside risk: Resumption of reinforcing negative momentum (< -5%) if global liquidity expands aggressively.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months), reflecting the use of 12-month moving averages and semi-annual momentum filters (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium, as the signal is currently in a transition phase with conflicting momentum indicators.

Macro Relevance:

  • This signal informs global financial conditions and dollar-denominated funding costs (1).
  • Economic mechanism: The current bearish level implies a tailwind for commodities and risk assets due to relative USD weakness (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typically conflicts with rising commodity prices or falling volatility when the USD enters a reinforcing Bullish regime (1).

Regime Context:

  • The Bearish regime has been persistent since April 2025 but is currently weakening in intensity (1).
  • The direction of recent change is stabilising/weakening as the 6-month change moves closer to zero (1).

Model Limitations:

  • Lagging behavior is inherent due to the use of a 12-month moving average and hysteresis bands (1).
  • Sensitivity to end-of-month price spikes can create false regime signals (1).

Data & References:

  • USD Index Signal (2026-03-01) (1). Data timeliness cannot be assessed.
  • Influential datapoints: Value_Monthly (119.49), USD_12Month_MA (120.27).
  • Integration with Credit Spreads and Federal Reserve Liquidity would improve interpretation of the current transition (1).
Investor Anatomy - Global Trade and Demand - Not Investment Advice
The information presented is for general informational purposes only and does not constitute financial or investment advice. It has been prepared without regard to individual objectives, financial situation, or needs. You should consider whether it is appropriate for your circumstances and seek independent advice where necessary.
